mentions.note: Raising phone prices $200 to pass on memory chip cost inflation from hyperscaler demand. Consumer electronics getting expensive; could become national political issue if trend continues.
Medical device sector weakness; device cohort in decline; same pressures as Boston Scientific.
Beer business just flat; not going to make you money. Only agave spirits has growth left in entire spirits complex; wife is in agave, that's the only game in town.
Doing incredibly well; fantastic growth company with great product (Claude). Put in for as much stock as you can when IPO happens. Next two AI IPOs are OpenAI and Anthropic.
Pre-IPO/private AI company; doing incredibly well with Claude product; fantastic growth company; Cramer wants club members to put in for as much stock as possible.
Stock down 9% despite being rarely down for the year. Precisely the level to put on 50% position now, then add if dips further next week. Steve Squeri doing dynamite job.
Medical device sector is from Hades; one good product didn't deliver sales. Entire device cohort (Intuitive, Abbott, Medtronic) crushed; just too hard to own.
Cramer's absolute favorite company; analyst day Wednesday; small-city convenience store model underappreciated by market; people don't know Casey story yet.
One of my absolute favorite companies; small-city convenience store model. Analyst day Wednesday could actually move the stock because people still don't know the Casey's story.
Well-run cruise operator; will report soon with first inkling of lower fuel cost and destination reopening impacts. Never told anyone not to buy it.
Shorts have played havoc long-term; interesting level to buy now. Pull trigger moment company reports quarter; watch for YoY comparisons weakness to create opportunity.
Same charge-down as American Express; credit card sector experiencing similar compression; not a concern.

Maybe the most exciting story of 2026 in technology and materials; pure-play semiconductor materials supplier. 17% organic growth; deep partnerships with Nvidia, Intel, Samsung, Micron. Materials innovation hidden hero of AI. New US facility already shipping, scaling next year.
Fabulous restaurant chain (Olive Garden); almost always surprises upside like clockwork. Gas prices coming down and that historically drives Darden performance for 25 years I followed it.
Recent LTL spin-off from FedEx; uniquely positioned to capture e-commerce growth and market share. Company cutting enormous costs while picking up share; self-driving trucks coming.
Recent LTL spinoff from FedEx; freight stocks on fire due to strong economy and e-commerce share gains; set to cut costs and gain market share; self-driving trucks coming.
Lower fuel costs and e-commerce tailwinds; CEO Raj Supermanian running juggernaut now after late Fred Smith; winning against UPS in trenches. Buy and put it away for long haul.

Now my favorite stock in charitable trust; memory chip shortage severe but CPU shortage coming next and that's Intel's wheelhouse. Expecting Apple deal to materialize; club aggressively recommended buying.
Robotics/engineering plays in favor; stock fine but a little too speculative for Cramer; hammered today but engineering market likes this sector.
Too much competition; used to be sole king but now there are three or four kings in surgical robotics space. Problem is too many competitors.
Well-run home builder reporting this week; housing dead in water due to high rates; only Toll Brothers (TOL) can handle situation with 25% cash-purchase model.
Medical device sector weakness; entire device cohort struggling and hard to own.
Down 42% from highs but could go down a few more bucks. AI-disrupted falling knife stock; don't try to catch falling knives, cells phones, safes, or manhole covers.
Stock crushed; Wall Street thinks Unilever food business acquisition is colossal overpay. Waiting to hear management's justification at earnings.
Government-backed rare earth materials play; traditionalist choice for mining and critical minerals exposure. Beat out Niakore which is too speculative.

Knicks/Rangers breakup play; trading at discount to sum-of-parts. Knicks alone worth $10.1B, Rangers $3.8B, but MSGS EV ~$10B. Spin-off catalyst could unlock $4B of value; analysts raising targets above $400.
Stock up 300% YTD; must beat and raise significantly beyond consensus to continue higher. Memory shortage severe; insane pricing power but cost passed to consumers via Apple.
Disappointing performance; discussed at club meeting yesterday. Giving company one more quarter for redemption; if they disappoint again, have to sell despite long hold period and sunk capital.
Jensen Wong saw data center demand coming in memory space; hyperscaler demand driving chip shortage; Cramer assumes Nvidia's dominance continues.
Not doing well; has some good people and products but not good balance sheet. Will probably be last in IPO shoot depending on deal terms; have to see where deal comes before jumping in.
Pre-IPO/private; good people and products but not good balance sheet; likely last in IPO queue; deal timing uncertain.
Quarters poorly received despite consistent beats and strong raises; pattern suggests AI disruption of payroll/HR possible. Not fighting the tape right now.
Long-term growth story; growth hasn't slowed, just the stock. Fine to own as long as you recognize it's multi-year bet, not a trading vehicle. Clubbed down today.
R2 launch last week but stock stalling despite recent pivot to lifestyle brand and insider buying. Losing too much money; not going to buy here.
Time to buy at current 18 level after rejection at 28 and pullback from highs. Fintech thesis intact; continue to believe this level is attractive entry.
AI light meme stock; $4 stock masquerading as $7 stock. Meme crowd bought SpaceX at $220; not interested in riding that wave.
Largest IPO in history recently completed; Cramer notes aftermath creates opportunity for sleepy interregnum in market.
Only home builder that can handle high-rate environment; 25% of homes bought with cash insulates from mortgage rate pressure. Only one that can handle the situation.
Down 50% from August highs; COVID stay-at-home farming trend completely over. People shifted to Amazon for basics and apparel. Can't think of reason to own it anymore.
Upscale cruise model (no kids, no gambling); all cruise lines well-run but Viking has best pricing power. Benefits from lower fuel costs and reopened destinations post-war.
